$comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the résumé. tools/build-resume.mjs generates resume.html (with embedded JSON-LD), resume.md, and resume.ld.json from this file; `--check` fails CI on drift. Edit here, never the generated files. Loosely follows the JSON Resume schema, with `dateRange` carrying the exact display string.

basics.name: Robert DeLanghe
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer
basics.summary: I ship systems where agents write the code — and I built the tooling to trust them in production.

summaryParagraphs: At Aura I ship agent-authored delivery in production. The instinct behind it is old — make invalid states unrepresentable, push correctness into the type system until violations can't compile — and agents are just where it turned urgent: orgs want them shipping like senior engineers and give them none of the safety rails.

So I built the rails — a contract-and-validation layer where every privileged effect is bound to a signed owner, enforced in CI. The harder, general version is out in the open, in prx and the bounded-systems libraries.

work.name: Bounded Systems
highlights: Designed a capability runtime — prx, guest-room, claude-box — where agents act only through the scoped door references they hold, every privileged effect is verified against its signed owner, and provenance is bound to an auditable chain (ocap-provenance).

work.name: Aura
highlights: Architected the contract-and-validation layer replacing Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan (the system behind inventory-purchasing): a compile-and-publish model where one source of truth generates every downstream surface — schemas, docs, tests, agent context — CI drift-gated so nothing falls out of sync.
Made the pipeline auditable by construction — provenance and contract checks gating every generated output, sign-off bound to an owner-role enforced in CI rather than to individuals.
Drove it as agent-authored delivery: every change moves through one auditable pipeline to a reviewed PR, applying the same provenance-and-contract discipline to the agents' own output.

work.name: L2L
highlights: Built static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline — blocking defects before merge, cutting critical bugs 20% and high-priority issues 25% across a 20-engineer team.

Shipped the frontend platform behind it — a design system (↓30% task lead time), a dashboard framework (widget builds a week → a day), and pagination + perf work that brought First Contentful Paint under a second.

work.name: Pioneer Works
highlights: A unified CRM + Square-API ETL — ↓80% payment-tracking time, P&L across 200+ events.

work.name: Kaleida Studio
highlights: Just-in-time production workflows — eliminated edition-numbering errors (10/month → 0).

work.name: The Prepared
highlights: A unified content pipeline — ↓70% curation time across 1000+ articles.

work.name: Recurse Center
highlights: Algebraic data types, typeclasses, and type-driven design — the same instinct now applied to security seams: invalid states unrepresentable, sign-off unforgeable.
Computational & parametric design — algorithmic design for digital fabrication.
